reasoning_model_1: The model accurately follows the user's command to read the sentence with a sigh. The subsequent delivery of the sentence 'this weather is so hot, I am melting' maintains a weary and exhausted tone that aligns perfectly with the semantic content. Although the model expands the contraction 'I\'m' to 'I am' (a minor deviation from the strict text script), this choice enhances the lethargic pacing suited to the emotion. The core paralinguistic feature (sigh) is vivid and clearly identifiable, meriting a high score.
reasoning_model_2: The model generates the speech 'This weather is so hot, I am melting' but fails to include the specific paralinguistic feature requested: a sigh. model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner as it successfully integrates the requested paralinguistic feature (a sigh) into the audio generation. The addition of 'Ah...' at the start effectively conveys the physical act of sighing, satisfying the 'Non-Linguistic Vocalizations' requirement. Model 2, in contrast, simply reads the text without any audible sigh or added vocalization, failing to meet the core instruction. Both models exhibited a minor text deviation by expanding the contraction 'I\'m', but Model 1's superior adherence to the feature control instruction makes it the better performance.
